card.intuition: A powerful multi-stage pipeline used to process, filter, and transform documents into aggregated results (like SQL's GROUP BY but far more flexible).
card.analogy: An assembly line in a factory: raw materials (documents) enter, and each station (stage) does one thing—adds a part, paints it, or counts it—until the finished product comes out at the end.
card.time_complexity: N/A (Dependent on pipeline complexity and indices)
card.space_complexity: N/A (100MB limit per stage unless allowDiskUse is true)
when_to_use: Grouping data by a specific field to calculate sums, averages, or counts
Reshaping documents (adding/removing fields) before sending them to the client
gotchas: Pipelines are ordered: filtering with $match should always come first to reduce the number of documents processed in later stages
$unwind can explode the data size significantly if an array has many elements, leading to memory issues

exercise.prompt: Complete the aggregation pipeline to find the average price of products in the 'Electronics' category.
hints: The value to average is the 'price' field
exercise.test_code:
assert pipeline[0].get("$match")
assert pipeline[1].get("$group")
assert pipeline[1]["$group"]["avgPrice"]["$avg"] == "$price"
print("PASS")
